content.full_text:
M. R. BEAL & COMPANY
Investment Bankers and Financial Mvisors
ta 5 \B t: l'rilt$
366 Madison Avenue
New York, NY 10017
212 983-3930
Fax 983-4539
February 1, 1990
Ms. Alice Hughes
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814
Dear Ms. Hughes:
Per our telephone conversation of Wednesday, January 31, it is my understanding
that I can give a gift of any amount to a legislator as long as it is reported by that
legislator. Thank you for your assistance and I look forward to receiving a written
confirmation of your opinion.
I would also appreciate a response to the questions listed below:
o 
Under what circumstances may a private firm, which does not have a
lobbyist working in California, provide an honorarium to a Member of
the California l-egislature?
o 
Under what circumstances may a private firm make a monetary gift to
a Member of the California lrgislature, which might be used to pay a
fine levied by a local or state entity?
o 
Under what circumstances may a private firm participate in the
establishment of an organization or committee designed to raise
money for the express purpose of making gitis to a legislator tbr the
express purpose of helping to pay fine levied by a local or state
government entity?
Your.earliest reply is appreciated as well as your consideration in addressing these
questlons.
$ipcerely, -.
ParnLPa**zL-
David P. Brand
Vice President

sections.question: Under what circumstances may a private firm, which does not have a lobbyist working in California, provide an honorarium to a Member of the California Legislature? Under what circumstances may a private firm make a monetary gift to a Member of the California Legislature, which might be used to pay a fine levied by a local or state entity? Under what circumstances may a private firm participate in the establishment of an organization or committee designed to raise money for the express purpose of making gifts to a legislator for the express purpose of helping to pay fine levied by a local or state government entity?
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks about the permissibility of providing honorariums and monetary gifts to California legislators, including gifts intended to help pay fines, and whether private firms can participate in establishing organizations to raise funds for such gifts to legislators.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or formal answer section present. The requester references a prior telephone conversation with the FPPC and requests written confirmation plus answers to three specific questions about legislator gifts.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Under what circumstances may a private firm, which does not have a lobbyist working in California, provide an honorarium to a Member of the California Legislature? Under what circumstances may a private firm make a monetary gift to a Member of the California Legislature, which might be used to pay a fine levied by a local or state entity? Under what circumstances may a private firm participate in the establishment of an organization or committee designed to raise money for the express purpose of making gifts to a legislator for the express purpose of helping to pay fine levied by a local or state government entity?

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from David P. Brand of M.R. Beal & Company asking the FPPC for written confirmation of a prior telephone conversation regarding gift restrictions for legislators, along with three specific questions about honorariums, monetary gifts, and participation in gift-raising organizations.
